2606.23566.title: LangMAP: A Language-Adaptive Approach to Tokenization
2606.23566.paper_summary: Language-specific tokenizers improve tokenization quality and the downstream performance of models on those languages. However, using such a tokenizer comes at a cost: either a new model must be trained from scratch, or the vocabulary of an existing pretrained model must be adapted. We propose Language-adaptive Maximum a Posteriori (LangMAP) Tokenization, a tokenization scheme that extends the UnigramLM algorithm to the multilingual setting, producing language-specific tokenization from a single shared vocabulary. Notably, LangMAP can be used when training a multilingual language model from scratch or to adapt a pretrained model's tokenizer to individual languages without changing its vocabulary. While language labels are required at training time, a key feature of the algorithm is that it then performs language-specific tokenization at inference without knowledge of the input's language. Across 14 open-source tokenizers, 9 natural languages, and 9 programming languages, LangMAP improves morphological boundary alignment and, for all coding languages tested, alignment with abstract syntax tree (AST) leaf boundaries. In fine-tuning experiments, results are mixed: LangMAP improves target-language grammatical acceptability (MultiBLiMP) on the languages tested; its benefits are less consistent on knowledge-related tasks (Global-PIQA, Belebele).
2606.23566.paper_summary_zh: 語言特定的分詞器改善了分詞質量及模型在這些語言上的下游表現。<lb/>然而，使用這樣的分詞器是有代價的：要麼必須從頭開始訓練一個新模型，要麼必須調整現有預訓練模型的詞彙表。<lb/>我們提出了語言自適應最大後驗（LangMAP）分詞，這是一種將UnigramLM算法擴展到多語言環境的分詞方案，從單一共享詞彙表中生成語言特定的分詞。<lb/>值得注意的是，LangMAP可以在從頭訓練多語言模型時使用，或將預訓練模型的分詞器適應於個別語言，而無需更改其詞彙表。<lb/>雖然在訓練時需要語言標籤，但該算法的一個關鍵特徵是，它在推斷時可以在不知道輸入語言的情況下執行語言特定的分詞。<lb/>在14個開源分詞器、9種自然語言和9種編程語言中，LangMAP改善了形態邊界對齊，並且在所有測試的編程語言中，與抽象語法樹（AST）葉邊界的對齊也得到了改善。<lb/>在微調實驗中，結果參差不齊：LangMAP改善了測試語言的目標語言語法可接受性（MultiBLiMP）；在知識相關任務（Global-PIQA，Belebele）中的效益則不那麼一致。
2606.23566.author: Clara Meister et.al.
2606.23566.authors: Clara Meister, Suchir Salhan, Andrzej Szablewski, Pietro Lesci, Paula Buttery, Tiago Pimentel
